input.background: Table 19.3 contains data on the acceptance of applications to graduate programs at the University of California Berkeley for 1973. Assuming men and women who applied for admission were equally well-qualified, one would expect an equal proportion of acceptance by gender.
input.question: Calculate the relative incidence of acceptance for males by major.

output.answer: Relative incidence of acceptance for males by major: RR_1 = 0.753; RR_2 = 0.838. Test for interaction: Hypotheses: H_0: RR_1 = RR_2 (no interaction) versus H_2: RR_1 ≠ RR_2 (interaction). Test statistic: X^2_sat.int = 0.136 with df = 1. P-value: P = 0.713 (no significant difference in strata-specific relative risk estimates; no significant interaction).

acceptance_status.description: Indicates whether the application was accepted (+) or not (-).

total_applicants_major_1_male.description: Total number of male applicants to Major 1.

accepted_major_1_male.description: Number of male applicants accepted to Major 1.

total_applicants_major_2_male.description: Total number of male applicants to Major 2.

accepted_major_2_male.description: Number of male applicants accepted to Major 2.

meta_info.caption: Sex bias in graduate school admissions?
